meta_title: For Students: idea validation to Solve no marketing skills (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome no marketing skills. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Idea Validation for Students: Solving No Marketing Skills
content.seo_description: Stop building in the dark. Learn to validate AI ideas without marketing skills using Antigravity and Claude Code in this intermediate student guide.
content.answer_block: You don't need a marketing budget; you need demand evidence. By using Antigravity to simulate user behavior and Claude Code to rapidly build landing page experiments, you can prove your idea's value before writing a single line of production code. Validate first, build second, and remove the guesswork forever.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks perfecting your architecture, only to launch to the sound of crickets. The 'build it and they will come' fallacy is the graveyard of student projects. Without a marketing background, you feel paralyzed, fearing that your technical brilliance will vanish into the void of an oversaturated internet.

But here is the secret: engineering is your greatest marketing tool. By treating your idea like a scientific hypothesis, you can use technical validation to force the market to tell you what they want. We are moving from 'hoping for users' to 'building for demand' using the raw power of AI-driven automation.

steps.title: Define the Value Hypothesis
steps.description: Articulate exactly who your user is and what specific pain point you are solving. If you can't state it in one sentence, you aren't ready to build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior product manager. Analyze my idea for [Insert Idea] and identify the three most common objections a potential user would have. Create a list of 5 questions to ask potential users to validate this pain point.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a markdown file named hypothesis.md documenting the user persona and the core problem being solved.'

steps.title: Simulate Market Sentiment
steps.description: Use AI to scrape and analyze existing discussions to see how people currently solve the problem and where they are failing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze public sentiment for [Insert Topic] on platforms like Reddit and X. Identify the top 3 frustrations users express about current solutions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Search for recent discussions on [Insert Topic] and summarize the top three unresolved user pain points into a report.txt file.'

steps.title: Build a Smoke Test Landing Page
steps.description: Create a high-converting landing page that captures emails to test interest before the product exists.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the HTML/Tailwind code for a high-converting landing page focusing on the value proposition identified in Step 1. Include a clear call to action for a waitlist.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a landing page index.html using Tailwind CSS and configure a simple form capture component.'

steps.title: Automate Outreach Scripts
steps.description: Draft personalized outreach messages that invite users to your waitlist without sounding like spam.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft 5 personalized, non-spammy outreach messages for LinkedIn and email that address the pain points identified in Step 2 and invite users to join the beta waitlist.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a file named outreach_templates.md containing 5 distinct messaging variations based on the user persona.'

steps.title: Analyze Conversion Metrics
steps.description: Evaluate the waitlist sign-up rate to determine if the product idea is worth the engineering investment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a framework for calculating 'Minimum Viable Traction' (MVT). What conversion rate should I look for to justify building the full MVP?
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a simple dashboard script to track waitlist signups and calculate conversion rate from traffic sources.'

pro_tips: Never build an authentication system until you have at least 50 emails on your waitlist.
Use 'The Mom Test' framework when talking to users—ask about their past behavior, not their future intentions.
If nobody complains about the problem on Reddit, the problem isn't painful enough to build a SaaS for.
Automate your feedback loop; use Claude Code to parse user emails into a prioritized feature request backlog automatically.
Treat your first 100 users as your product advisory board, not just customers.
content.next_steps: Once you hit your MVT goal, transition from validation to building your MVP using the feedback gathered from your waitlist users.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to validate any idea before wasting months on code that nobody wants. Stop guessing and start building with data-backed confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
